title: mildly spiced spanish chicken paella
description: a peppery and tender date-night spanish dish featuring capers and confit-tomato. low-sodium friendly and cooked in a copper pot.
instructions: heat the olive oil in a copper pot over medium-high heat. add the chicken breast and cook until browned.
remove the chicken from the pot and set aside. add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
add the arborio rice and cook for 1 minute. stir constantly to prevent burning.
add the confit tomato, chicken broth, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot. stir to combine.
add the browned chicken breast back to the pot and stir to combine.
bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 15 minutes. stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
add the chopped capers and continue to cook for an additional 5 minutes. stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
reduce heat to low and cover the pot with a lid. cook for an additional 10 minutes.
remove the lid and fluff the rice with a fork. serve hot with lemon wedges on the side.
